description: Author the 'center' concept as a living spec section in docs/architecture.md (design = living document). A 'center' is the shared hub a federation of landings points at: it carries the shared store branch (balls/tasks) that many landings sync, and is the target/source for capability publishing via bl install (--to <center> publishes a recommendation = dangling bin/; --from <center> adopts, §6). Define: what a center is and how it differs from a landing; store-branch sharing across landings (§12 store pointer); install publish/adopt against a center; and how §16 migration cutover targets a center (the coordinated, non-force-rewrite push of the shared store). Deliverable is the spec section edited like code, not a growing task body. Spun out of bl-1280's reframe: the migration workflow + center are core machinery delivered FIRST; bl-1280 (forge/issues port) is the downstream demo.
title: Center spec: shared hub for federation, capability publishing + migration cutover (new §)
